executiveSummary: PRD-004 (Frontend Design Refinement — Impeccable Pass) is substantially complete. All four user stories (US-001 through US-004) have been implemented: the audit skill was run and all 7 critical/high issues resolved (radiogroup role, contrast ratio upgrade to #7c3aed, role='log' on chat messages, progressbar ARIA, 44×44 touch targets, focus-visible on download link); the distill, harden, polish, and animate skills were applied and their outputs are confirmed in the codebase. One functional requirement is non-compliant: FR-3 (teach-impeccable must be run before the audit pass) has no evidence of execution — no impeccable config file exists in the repository. All other functional requirements comply: skills were applied in the correct order (FR-1), build succeeds cleanly (FR-5), and no new runtime dependencies were introduced (FR-4).

verificationByFR.notes: Skills applied in order: audit (US-001) → distill (US-002) → harden (US-003) → polish+animate (US-004), matching the required sequence. Confirmed by prototype-creation_004.json entries.

Audit and distill recommendations were reviewed and many applied (all 7 critical/high issues fixed). However, there is no explicit record of rejected recommendations for the distill pass — no distill-specific artifact documents which suggestions were declined.
No evidence that teach-impeccable was run before the audit pass. No .impeccable config file or any teach-impeccable artifact found anywhere in the repository.
package.json dependencies unchanged: only react and react-dom as runtime dependencies. No new runtime packages added.
bun run build completes successfully: 41 modules transformed, CSS 8.82 kB, JS 153.70 kB, exit code 0.

verificationByUS.notes: AC01: audit skill ran covering visual hierarchy, accessibility (contrast, keyboard nav, ARIA), responsive behavior, error states. AC02: all 7 critical/high issues resolved in code (C-01 radiogroup, H-01 contrast #7c3aed, H-02 role=log, H-03 progressbar ARIA, H-04 send 44px, H-05 remove-image 44px, H-06 download focus-visible). AC03: report saved to .agents/flow/it_000047_audit-report_001.md.

AC01: distill skill applied (prototype-creation_004 US-002 completed). AC02: chat input, media selector, and parameter panel are visually distinct in the implemented CSS. AC03: no decorative-only elements remain per code review.

AC01: ChatBubble uses max-width:80% + word-break:break-word — long prompts wrap without breaking layout. AC02: 30-second SSE timeout implemented in App.tsx (setTimeout 30_000ms → status:'timeout' bubble). AC03: Empty state div with data-testid='chat-empty' shows 'Type a prompt and press Generate to begin.' AC04: focus-visible styles on textarea, send button, upload button, remove button, media-type buttons, download link, and parameter inputs. AC05: server errors surfaced via status:'error' on the assistant bubble with full error text.

AC01: polish applied — consistent spacing and typography confirmed. AC02: animations implemented: bubbleIn (entrance), progressPulse (progress bar), mediaReveal (media), spin (loading indicator). AC03: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) disables all 4 animations (bubble, spinner, progress bar fill, media reveal). AC04: all animations are CSS-only and non-blocking.

minorObservations: role='log' is used on the chat messages container (H-02 fix). Per ARIA spec, role='log' has implicit aria-live='polite' — this is correct, but some screen reader implementations may benefit from an explicit aria-live='polite' attribute for maximum compatibility.

The aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax attributes are absent from the progressbar element (only aria-valuenow is present). ARIA 1.1 recommends all three attributes for progressbar roles. Low severity since aria-valuemin defaults to 0 and aria-valuemax defaults to 100.
No distill-specific rejection log exists. FR-2 requires that rejected recommendations are noted; distill recommendations applied without a documented rationale for any rejections.

teach-impeccable (FR-3) was not run. This means the design context file (.impeccable.md or equivalent) was never established, so the audit skill ran without project-specific design guidelines. This may affect the quality of the audit output.

The removeBtn touch target in ImageUpload.module.css was expanded to 44×44px (H-05 fix), but this is achieved via width/height on the button element. Verify that the actual clickable area is 44px — if the image thumbnail is sized differently, padding may be needed to maintain the target.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: PRD-004 is ready to approve with one minor remediation required: FR-3 (teach-impeccable) should be run retroactively to establish the project design context file. This is a low-cost step and does not require revisiting any implemented code. FR-2's missing distill rejection log is a documentation gap — recommended to add a brief note in the audit.md file listing which distill suggestions were deferred. All implemented changes are production-quality: accessibility issues are resolved, animations respect prefers-reduced-motion, the build is clean, and no new runtime dependencies were introduced. Recommend: (1) run teach-impeccable and commit the resulting config, (2) add missing aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax attributes to the progressbar element, (3) document distill rejections in audit.md, then mark the iteration as approved.
